req.title: Manager views document
req.statement: An authenticated manager shall be able to view a document through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: R-WEB-032 demands: (1) a happy-path where an authenticated manager successfully views a document and an audit record is written; (2) negative paths covering unauthenticated access (401) and forbidden role (403); (3) boundary coverage around mandatory field validation (empty vs. minimal-valid document id). Tests are scoped to the /documents UI screen of hr-app. Each test uses fixtures for session setup and helper stubs for audit verification, keeping executables under 40 lines. Heuristics applied: EP for the standard manager-views-document partition, NEG for auth/role failures, BVA for the field-length boundary, STC for the state transition from unauthenticated to authenticated, and DT for the role-permission decision table.

tests.name: test_manager_views_document_success
preconditions: User is authenticated as a manager
A valid document exists with known document_id
actions: Select the target document by document_id
expected: Response body contains entity id matching document_id
Audit record is written for the view action
trace: R-WEB-032: positive outcome — document viewed, audit written, 200/201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_manager_views_document_success(
    manager_session,        # <fixture>: authenticated manager HTTP session
    existing_document_id,   # <fixture>: pre-seeded document id
    assert_audit_record,    # <helper_fn>: polls audit log for action+entity
):
    resp = manager_session.get(
        "/documents",
        params={"document_id": existing_document_id},
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), (
        f"Expected 200/201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    )
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("id") == existing_document_id
    assert assert_audit_record(
        action="view_document",
        entity_id=existing_document_id,
    ), "Audit record not found after successful document view"

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_view_document
preconditions: A valid document exists with known document_id
actions: Send GET /documents with document_id but without auth token
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_view_document(
    anon_session,           # <fixture>: unauthenticated HTTP session
    existing_document_id,   # <fixture>: pre-seeded document id
    assert_no_audit_record, # <helper_fn>: asserts absence of audit entry
):
    resp = anon_session.get(
        "/documents",
        params={"document_id": existing_document_id},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 401, (
        f"Expected 401, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    )
    assert assert_no_audit_record(
        action="view_document",
        entity_id=existing_document_id,
    ), "Unexpected audit record found for unauthenticated request"

tests.name: test_forbidden_role_cannot_view_document
preconditions: User is authenticated but has role 'employee' (not manager)
A valid document exists with known document_id
actions: Navigate to /documents as an employee-role user
Attempt to view the target document by document_id
trace: R-WEB-032: error pathway — 403 if role forbidden
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_forbidden_role_cannot_view_document(
    employee_session,       # <fixture>: authenticated employee (non-manager) session
    existing_document_id,   # <fixture>: pre-seeded document id
    assert_no_audit_record, # <helper_fn>: asserts absence of audit entry
):
    resp = employee_session.get(
        "/documents",
        params={"document_id": existing_document_id},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 403, (
        f"Expected 403, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    )
    assert assert_no_audit_record(
        action="view_document",
        entity_id=existing_document_id,
    ), "Unexpected audit record found for forbidden-role request"

tests.name: test_view_document_with_empty_document_id_returns_400
preconditions: User is authenticated as a manager
actions: Submit a view request with document_id set to empty string (boundary: below minimum valid length)
expected: Response body contains a validation error message referencing document_id
trace: R-WEB-032: error pathway — 400 on validation error
R-WEB-032: mandatory fields must be validated
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_view_document_with_empty_document_id_returns_400(
    manager_session,        # <fixture>: authenticated manager HTTP session
    assert_no_audit_record, # <helper_fn>: asserts absence of audit entry
):
    EMPTY_ID = ""
    resp = manager_session.get(
        "/documents",
        params={"document_id": EMPTY_ID},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 400, (
        f"Expected 400, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    )
    body = resp.json()
    errors = body.get("errors", body.get("detail", ""))
    assert "document_id" in str(errors).lower(), (
        f"Validation error did not reference document_id: {errors}"
    )
    assert assert_no_audit_record(
        action="view_document",
        entity_id=EMPTY_ID,
    ), "Unexpected audit record found for invalid document_id request"

tests.name: test_manager_view_document_state_transition_unauthenticated_to_authenticated
preconditions: A valid document exists with known document_id
actions: Authenticate as manager to obtain a valid session token
Re-request /documents with the new token and document_id
expected: After authentication, second request returns 200 or 201
Audit record is written only after the authenticated request
trace: R-WEB-032: state — unauthenticated (401) transitions to authenticated-manager (200/201 + audit)
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_manager_view_document_state_transition_unauthenticated_to_authenticated(
    anon_session,           # <fixture>: unauthenticated HTTP session
    authenticate_as_manager,# <helper_fn>: performs login, returns authed session
    existing_document_id,   # <fixture>: pre-seeded document id
    assert_audit_record,    # <helper_fn>: polls audit log for action+entity
):
    pre_resp = anon_session.get(
        "/documents",
        params={"document_id": existing_document_id},
    )
    assert pre_resp.status_code == 401, (
        f"Pre-auth: expected 401, got {pre_resp.status_code}"
    )
    authed_session = authenticate_as_manager()
    post_resp = authed_session.get(
        "/documents",
        params={"document_id": existing_document_id},
    )
    assert post_resp.status_code in (200, 201), (
        f"Post-auth: expected 200/201, got {post_resp.status_code}"
    )
    assert post_resp.json().get("id") == existing_document_id
    assert assert_audit_record(
        action="view_document",
        entity_id=existing_document_id,
    ), "Audit record missing after authenticated document view"
